basic_details.job_title: UKMSSB Recruitment 2026 - Apply Online for 54 Professor and Associate Professor Posts
basic_details.department: Medical Education Department, Government of Uttarakhand
basic_details.short_information: The Uttarakhand Medical Service Selection Board (UKMSSB) has released a detailed advertisement for the Professor/Associate Professor Examination 2026, inviting online applications for direct recruitment to 8 Professor and 46 Associate Professor posts in Government Medical Colleges under the Medical Education Department, Government of Uttarakhand. Online applications open on 09 July 2026 and close on 29 July 2026. Eligible doctors holding a postgraduate medical qualification as per NMC-TEQ Regulations can apply through the official portal at www.ukmssb.org. This article covers the department-wi

important_dates.correction_date: 31 July 2026 to 03 August 2026, up to 5:00 PM (one-time, 4-day window)

application_fee.details: Fees can be paid only online, through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. Candidates under the Uttarakhand Ex-Servicemen sub-category will pay the fee applicable to their original category (General/SC/ST/OBC). The fee, once submitted, is non-refundable under any circumstances, including if the application is later cancelled and resubmitted, or if the application/candidature is rejected for not meeting eligibility conditions.

age_limit.details: The cutoff date for age calculation is 01 July 2025. For Uttarakhand's Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and other notified categories, the upper age limit will be higher by as many years as specified in the applicable rules. | SC/ST/OBC (Uttarakhand): 5 years relaxation in maximum age (as per Government Order No. 1399/XXX(2)/2005 dated 21 May 2005). Dependents of Uttarakhand Freedom Fighters: 5 years relaxation (Government Order No. 1244/XXX(2)/2005 dated 21 May 2005). Divyangjan (Persons with Disability) of Uttarakhand: 5 years relaxation (Government Order No. 1244/XXX(2)/2005 dated 21 May 2005). Ex-Servicemen of Uttarakhand: As per Notification No. 6/1/72-Karmik-2 dated 25 April 1977, actual age is reduced by the period of armed forces service, provided the resultant age does not exceed the prescribed maximum age limit for the post by more than 3 years. Orphan Children: Age relaxation provision as per Uttarakhand Government letter No. 11/XXXI(2)/2022-30(2)2019 dated 16 February 2022.

qualification.details: The post is gazetted and carries a contributory pension. | Qualification is as per the latest NMC-TEQ Regulations - Minimum Qualifications for Teachers in Medical Institutions Regulations, dated 30 June 2025. | All else being equal, preference in direct recruitment will be given to candidates who have: | Served a minimum of 2 years in the Territorial Army, OR Obtained a National Cadet Corps (NCC) 'B' Certificate | Candidates must hold a valid/renewed registration certificate with the medical council of any state in the country, up to the last date of online application. Note: For non-clinical subjects, candidates holding an M.Sc (Medical)/Ph.D medical degree are not required to have Medical Council registration. | The candidate must be a citizen of India; or a Tibetan refugee who came to India before 1 January 1962 with the intention of permanently settling in India; or a person of Indian origin who has migrated from Pakistan, Burma, Sri Lanka, or the East African countries of Kenya, Uganda, and the United Republic of Tanzania, with the intention of permanently settling in India. | The candidate's character must be suitable for government service. Persons dismissed from service under Central/State Government or a local authority/corporation under their ownership/control are not eligible. A male candidate with more than one living wife, or a female candidate with more than one living husband, is not eligible unless the Government is satisfied there is a special reason to grant exemption. The candidate must be physically and mentally fit, free of any physical defect likely to interfere with efficient discharge of duties. A fitness certificate from the prescribed Medical Board is required before appointment to a gazetted post. Physical fitness for PWD candidates will be assessed as per their disability category certificate from an authorised medical practitioner.

salary_details.details: As per Government Order No. 79293/XXVIII(5)/2022-(E-22311) dated 28 November 2022, a 50% allowance under the Medical Teachers Deficiency Compensatory Scheme is admissible for postings in hilly areas, namely Srinagar and Almora.

selection_process: Sl. No. Component Evaluation Basis Marks
1 Paper Publication 5 marks per publication, maximum 20 marks (valid as per standards prescribed by MCI/NMC) 20

2 Experience 4 marks per completed year, maximum 20 marks (only experience at State/Central/Autonomous Government hospitals or medical colleges is valid; private sector experience is not counted; marks are payable only for experience beyond the minimum prescribed qualifying experience) 20
3 Additional Educational Qualification M.Ch/DM/DNB Super Specialty 10

Paper publications will be considered valid as per the notifications applicable at the time they were approved by MCI/NMC. Experience is counted only in complete years (365 days); any period of absence is excluded from the experience calculation. Only the experience

and additional qualification details entered in the online application will be considered - claims made later during document verification that were not entered online will not be considered by the Board.
how_to_apply: Click on "Apply Now" against "Professor/Associate Professor Examination-2026 (for Medical College)."
Click "Candidate Register Here" to complete your registration using a valid e-mail ID and mobile number.
After registration, you will receive a registration number and password via e-mail and SMS.

Log in using the registration number and password, and complete the application in five steps: Step 1: Registration and Personal Details Step 2: Education, Experience, Paper Publication and Council Details Step 3: Upload Photo, Signature and Documents Step 4: Make Payment Step 5: Print Application Form
Step 2: Education, Experience, Paper Publication and Council Details
Step 3: Upload Photo, Signature and Documents
Carefully verify all entries. If satisfied that all details are correct, click "I Agree" at the end of the form to finalise submission.
Pay the application fee through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. Applications will not be accepted without fee payment.
Download and print a copy of the submitted application form for future reference.
Only online applications will be accepted; offline or hard-copy applications will not be considered. Incomplete online applications will also not be accepted.
important_instructions: Candidates must ensure they hold all mandatory educational, category, and reservation-related certificates by the last date of the online application. For educational qualification, the date on the mark sheet (Marksheet Issuing Date) will be considered. The Board does not advise candidates on their eligibility; candidates should study the notification carefully and apply only if satisfied that they meet all conditions. Issue of an admit card does not mean the candidature has been finally confirmed by the Board. If found ineligible at any stage, the candidature will be cancelled, even after final selection. No change is permitted in any detail shown in the submitted online application after the correction window closes, including the issue date and number of category/sub-category certificates. Signatures made at all stages - application form, attendance sheets, and correspondence with the Board - must be identical; any discrepancy may lead to cancellation of candidature. The date of birth recorded in the High School Certificate will be treated as final. Left thumb impression (for male candidates) and right thumb impression (for female candidates) will be required at the time of document verification. In case two or more candidates score equal marks, the candidate holding preferential qualification will be considered senior; if neither holds preferential qualification, the older candidate will be considered senior; if age is also equal, seniority will be determined alphabetically by name. Selected candidates must undergo the required medical/health examination as per rules before appointment. Spreading misleading information about the examination will attract action under the Uttarakhand Competitive Examination (Measures for Prevention and Prohibition of Unfair Means in Recruitment) Act, 2023.

content_sections.heading: UKMSSB Professor/Associate Professor Recruitment 2026: Quick Overview

rows.details: Professor/Associate Professor Examination - 2026 (for Government Medical Colleges)
Medical Education Department, Government of Uttarakhand
100 marks - Paper Publication, Experience, Additional Educational Qualification, Interview

content_sections.heading: UKMSSB Professor/Associate Professor Recruitment 2026: Important Dates

rows.event: Last Date to Submit Online Application
Last Date for Fee Payment (Net Banking/Debit/Credit Card/UPI)

rows.date: 31 July 2026 to 03 August 2026, up to 5:00 PM (one-time, 4-day window)

content_sections.heading: UKMSSB Recruitment 2026: Vacancy Details
content_sections.text: A total of 54 posts are available - 8 Professor and 46 Associate Professor posts. The Board has clarified that the number of vacancies may increase or decrease. Note: As per Uttarakhand Government Order No. 79293/XXVIII(5)/2022-(E-22311) dated 28 November 2022, a 50% allowance is admissible for hilly areas - i.e., Srinagar/Almora - under the Medical Teachers Deficiency Compensatory Scheme. Note: As per the letter of the Director, Directorate of Medical Education, Uttarakhand (No. 8375/चि0शि0/03(मेडिकल)/09/2019-25 dated 09 February 2026), in the Anatomy, Physiology, Biochemistry, Microbiology, and Pharmacology faculties, non-medical (non-doctor) candidates will be selected only if medical teacher candidates (MD/MS/DNB) as per the latest MCI/NMC norms are not available, so as to maintain parity for non-medico posts.

content_sections.heading: Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: The post is gazetted and carries a contributory pension. Qualification is as per the latest NMC-TEQ Regulations - Minimum Qualifications for Teachers in Medical Institutions Regulations, dated 30 June 2025. All else being equal, preference in direct recruitment will be given to candidates who have: Served a minimum of 2 years in the Territorial Army, OR Obtained a National Cadet Corps (NCC) 'B' Certificate Candidates must hold a valid/renewed registration certificate with the medical council of any state in the country, up to the last date of online application. Note: For non-clinical subjects, candidates holding an M.Sc (Medical)/Ph.D medical degree are not required to have Medical Council registration. The candidate must be a citizen of India; or a Tibetan refugee who came to India before 1 January 1962 with the intention of permanently settling in India; or a person of Indian origin who has migrated from Pakistan, Burma, Sri Lanka, or the East African countries of Kenya, Uganda, and the United Republic of Tanzania, with the intention of permanently settling in India. The candidate's character must be suitable for government service. Persons dismissed from service under Central/State Government or a local authority/corporation under their ownership/control are not eligible. A male candidate with more than one living wife, or a female candidate with more than one living husband, is not eligible unless the Government is satisfied there is a special reason to grant exemption. The candidate must be physically and mentally fit, free of any physical defect likely to interfere with efficient discharge of duties. A fitness certificate from the prescribed Medical Board is required before appointment to a gazetted post. Physical fitness for PWD candidates will be assessed as per their disability category certificate from an authorised medical practitioner.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit
content_sections.text: The cutoff date for age calculation is 01 July 2025. For Uttarakhand's Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, and other notified categories, the upper age limit will be higher by as many years as specified in the applicable rules. SC/ST/OBC (Uttarakhand): 5 years relaxation in maximum age (as per Government Order No. 1399/XXX(2)/2005 dated 21 May 2005). Dependents of Uttarakhand Freedom Fighters: 5 years relaxation (Government Order No. 1244/XXX(2)/2005 dated 21 May 2005). Divyangjan (Persons with Disability) of Uttarakhand: 5 years relaxation (Government Order No. 1244/XXX(2)/2005 dated 21 May 2005). Ex-Servicemen of Uttarakhand: As per Notification No. 6/1/72-Karmik-2 dated 25 April 1977, actual age is reduced by the period of armed forces service, provided the resultant age does not exceed the prescribed maximum age limit for the post by more than 3 years. Orphan Children: Age relaxation provision as per Uttarakhand Government letter No. 11/XXXI(2)/2022-30(2)2019 dated 16 February 2022.

content_sections.heading: Pay Scale / Salary
content_sections.text: As per Government Order No. 79293/XXVIII(5)/2022-(E-22311) dated 28 November 2022, a 50% allowance under the Medical Teachers Deficiency Compensatory Scheme is admissible for postings in hilly areas, namely Srinagar and Almora.

content_sections.heading: Reservation Policy
content_sections.text: Reservation is available only to holders of a Uttarakhand permanent resident certificate. Categories eligible for vertical/horizontal reservation as per prevailing Uttarakhand Government orders include: Scheduled Castes, Scheduled Tribes, Other Backward Classes, Economically Weaker Sections, Ex-Servicemen, dependents of Freedom Fighters, Divyangjan (PWD), Uttarakhand Women, orphan children of Uttarakhand, Uttarakhand agitators (or their dependents), and skilled sportspersons. PWD reservation requires a minimum of 40% disability in the relevant category. For Professor/Associate Professor posts under the Medical Education Department, only the "One Leg Affected (O.L.)" disability category is eligible for reservation, as per Government Order No. 48/XVII-A-3/2023-01(11)/Vi.K./2017 dated 05 June 2023; applications from other disability categories will not be accepted. Ex-servicemen reservation is admissible only to personnel themselves who have retired/been discharged from the Army, as per Government Order No. 133/XXXVI(3)2009/14(1)/2009 dated 16 March 2009; their dependents are not eligible for this reservation. SC/ST candidates applying under the reserved category must submit a caste certificate issued by a competent Uttarakhand authority. OBC candidates must submit an OBC certificate issued as per Government Order No. 310/XVII-2/16-02(OBC)/2012 dated 26 February 2016; the certificate must not be older than 3 years from the last date of application submission, and must have been issued on or after 30 July 2023 and before the application deadline of 29 July 2026 to be considered valid. EWS reservation is admissible only to permanent residents of Uttarakhand. Candidates applying under this category are not entitled to any relaxation in the maximum age limit. Only EWS certificates issued for FY 2026-27, based on FY 2025-26 income, will be considered valid. Reservation for orphan children applies to children residing in registered voluntary/government homes under the Department of Women Empowerment and Child Development, Uttarakhand, with no available information about their parents; a 5% horizontal reservation applies as per the applicable rules. Skilled sportspersons who have won medals or participated at recognised international/national levels are entitled to 4% horizontal reservation, subject to conditions specified in the notification. Reservation benefits are available only once a candidate marks the relevant reserved category/sub-category in the online application and uploads the supporting certificate; no reservation claim will be entertained afterward, and no change in the declared category/sub-category will be permitted later.

content_sections.heading: No Objection Certificate (NOC)
content_sections.text: Candidates who are regularly working in Central or State Government service as on the date of application must submit a departmental No Objection Certificate from their concerned department.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process (Total 100 Marks)
content_sections.text: Paper publications will be considered valid as per the notifications applicable at the time they were approved by MCI/NMC. Experience is counted only in complete years (365 days); any period of absence is excluded from the experience calculation. Only the experience, paper publication, and additional qualification details entered in the online application will be considered - claims made later during document verification that were not entered online will not be considered by the Board.

rows.evaluation_basis: 5 marks per publication, maximum 20 marks (valid as per standards prescribed by MCI/NMC)

4 marks per completed year, maximum 20 marks (only experience at State/Central/Autonomous Government hospitals or medical colleges is valid; private sector experience is not counted; marks are payable only for experience beyond the minimum prescribed qualifying experience)

content_sections.heading: Required Documents
content_sections.text: Candidates must upload a single combined PDF file (200 KB to 5 MB) containing self-attested copies of the following documents: High School Certificate Intermediate Certificate Domicile of Uttarakhand (if any) Category Certificate (SC/ST/OBC/EWS) Sub-category certificates: Dependent of Freedom Fighter (DFF)/Ex-Army/Differently Abled (PH)/Orphan Children of Uttarakhand State/Skilled Sportsperson/Uttarakhand Agitator or Agitator's dependent (if any) UG Mark Sheet (all years/semesters) UG Degree Certificate PG Mark Sheet (all years/semesters) PG Degree Certificate PhD Degree (for non-clinical candidates, if any) DM/M.Ch/DNB Super Specialty certificate (if any) Experience Certificates Paper Publications (only the first page of each paper) Medical Council Registration Certificate (with validity) NCC/Territorial Army Certificate (if any) Candidates must also upload a scanned photograph (20 KB to 50 KB) and scanned signature (5 KB to 20 KB) separately, in addition to the combined document PDF.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee
content_sections.text: Fees can be paid only online, through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. Candidates under the Uttarakhand Ex-Servicemen sub-category will pay the fee applicable to their original category (General/SC/ST/OBC). The fee, once submitted, is non-refundable under any circumstances, including if the application is later cancelled and resubmitted, or if the application/candidature is rejected for not meeting eligibility conditions.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for UKMSSB Professor/Associate Professor Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Visit the official website: www.ukmssb.org . Click on "Apply Now" against "Professor/Associate Professor Examination-2026 (for Medical College)." Click "Candidate Register Here" to complete your registration using a valid e-mail ID and mobile number. After registration, you will receive a registration number and password via e-mail and SMS. Log in using the registration number and password, and complete the application in five steps: Step 1: Registration and Personal Details Step 2: Education, Experience, Paper Publication and Council Details Step 3: Upload Photo, Signature and Documents Step 4: Make Payment Step 5: Print Application Form Carefully verify all entries. If satisfied that all details are correct, click "I Agree" at the end of the form to finalise submission. Pay the application fee through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. Applications will not be accepted without fee payment. Download and print a copy of the submitted application form for future reference. Only online applications will be accepted; offline or hard-copy applications will not be considered. Incomplete online applications will also not be accepted.

content_sections.heading: Edit/Correction Process
content_sections.text: The edit/correction link for the submitted application will open one working day after the online application closes on 29 July 2026, and remain open for 4 days - from 31 July 2026 to 03 August 2026, up to 5:00 PM. Only candidates who have completed the application process can log in using their registration number and password to make corrections. Candidates can amend any field except mobile number and e-mail ID. Entries as edited during this window will be treated as final; data as of the original application closing date will otherwise be considered for processing timelines. If a candidate changes their category/sub-category during correction (for example, from Unreserved to SC), the fee difference, if any, will not be refunded. No request for correction/modification of any entry or claim will be considered by the Board once this correction window closes.

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions
content_sections.text: Candidates must ensure they hold all mandatory educational, category, and reservation-related certificates by the last date of the online application. For educational qualification, the date on the mark sheet (Marksheet Issuing Date) will be considered. The Board does not advise candidates on their eligibility; candidates should study the notification carefully and apply only if satisfied that they meet all conditions. Issue of an admit card does not mean the candidature has been finally confirmed by the Board. If found ineligible at any stage, the candidature will be cancelled, even after final selection. No change is permitted in any detail shown in the submitted online application after the correction window closes, including the issue date and number of category/sub-category certificates. Signatures made at all stages - application form, attendance sheets, and correspondence with the Board - must be identical; any discrepancy may lead to cancellation of candidature. The date of birth recorded in the High School Certificate will be treated as final. Left thumb impression (for male candidates) and right thumb impression (for female candidates) will be required at the time of document verification. In case two or more candidates score equal marks, the candidate holding preferential qualification will be considered senior; if neither holds preferential qualification, the older candidate will be considered senior; if age is also equal, seniority will be determined alphabetically by name. Selected candidates must undergo the required medical/health examination as per rules before appointment. Spreading misleading information about the examination will attract action under the Uttarakhand Competitive Examination (Measures for Prevention and Prohibition of Unfair Means in Recruitment) Act, 2023.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for UKMSSB Professor/Associate Professor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application closes on 29 July 2026 (Wednesday) at 5:00 PM. The fee must also be paid by the same date and time.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in UKMSSB Professor/Associate Professor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 54 total vacancies - 8 for Professor and 46 for Associate Professor - though the notification states the number of vacancies may increase or decrease.

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for UKMSSB Professor and Associate Professor posts?
faq.answer: As on 01 July 2025, the minimum age is 35 years for Professor and 30 years for Associate Professor, with a maximum age of 62 years for both posts. Age relaxation of 5 years is available for SC/ST/OBC, freedom fighter dependents, and Divyangjan candidates of Uttarakhand, as detailed in the Age Limit section above.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for UKMSSB Professor/Associate Professor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹2,000 for Unreserved and Uttarakhand OBC candidates, and ₹1,000 for EWS, Uttarakhand SC, Uttarakhand ST, and Uttarakhand PWD candidates, payable online only.

faq.question: Q5. What is the pay scale for UKMSSB Professor and Associate Professor posts?
faq.answer: Professor is paid ₹1,44,200 - ₹2,18,200 (Level-14), and Associate Professor is paid ₹1,31,100 - ₹3,16,600 (Level-13(A)). A 50% Medical Teachers Deficiency Compensatory Scheme allowance is additionally admissible for postings in Srinagar/Almora.

faq.question: Q6. How is selection done for UKMSSB Professor/Associate Professor posts?
faq.answer: Selection is based on 100 total marks: up to 20 marks for paper publications, up to 20 marks for relevant government-sector experience, 10 marks for additional educational qualification (DM/M.Ch/DNB Super Specialty), and 50 marks for the interview.

faq.question: Q7. Is Medical Council registration compulsory for all candidates?
faq.answer: Medical Council registration is compulsory for clinical subject candidates. It is not mandatory for candidates in non-clinical subjects holding an M.Sc (Medical)/Ph.D medical degree.

faq.question: Q8. Can I edit my application after submission?
faq.answer: Yes, but only during the designated correction window from 31 July 2026 to 03 August 2026. You can edit any field except your registered mobile number and e-mail ID. No corrections will be entertained after this window closes.

faq.question: Q9. Is reservation available to candidates from outside Uttarakhand?
faq.answer: No. Reservation benefits under this recruitment are available only to candidates holding a Uttarakhand permanent resident certificate.

faq.question: Q10. When will the interview for UKMSSB Professor/Associate Professor Recruitment 2026 be held?
faq.answer: The interview and document verification are proposed to be held in November-December 2026. The exact schedule with roll numbers will be published on the official UKMSSB website.

faq.question: What is the last date to apply for UKMSSB Professor/Associate Professor Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application closes on 29 July 2026 (Wednesday) at 5:00 PM. The fee must also be paid by the same date and time.
